blocks.text: Draft me a confidentiality clause for a Series B term sheet between a UK manufacturing company and a US lead investor. Standard market terms; mutual; 3-year survival.

blocks.text:
This is a clause-drafting task, not a document review — no playbook or skill triggers. I'll draft in plain text.

---

**Confidentiality Clause — Series B Term Sheet**

> **Confidentiality**
>
> Each party (the "Receiving Party") agrees to hold in confidence all Confidential Information of the other party (the "Disclosing Party") and shall not use such Confidential Information for any purpose except in connection with the contemplated Series B funding (the "Purpose"). The Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, except to its legal counsel, financial advisors, and prospective investors in connection with the Purpose, provided that such recipients are bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than those contained herein.
>
> "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party, whether orally, in writing, or by inspection, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably ought to be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure. Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available through no breach of this provision by the Receiving Party; (b) was lawfully known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully obtained from a third party without restriction on disclosure.
>
> Notwithstanding the foregoing, each party may disclose the existence and general nature of the contemplated transaction to the extent required by applicable securities law or regulation, provided that the disclosing party promptly notifies the other party of such disclosure requirement and uses commercially reasonable efforts to seek confidential treatment of the terms hereof to theextent permitted by law.
>
> The confidentiality obligations under this clause shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure of the applicable Confidential Information. All Confidential Information remains the property of the Disclosing Party, and no licence or rights thereto is granted by disclosure.

---

**Notes:**
- **Mutual** — both parties bound symmetrically.
- **Standard exclusions** — publicly available, prior knowledge, independent development, lawful third-party disclosure.
- **Permitted disclosures** — legal counsel, financial advisors, prospective investors (standard carve-out for professional advisors bound to confidentiality).
- **Securities-law carve-out** — needed for a Series B; allows disclosure as required by SEC/FCA rules with prompt notice and efforts to seek confidential treatment.
- **3-year survival** — standard for term sheets.

Want me to adjust the carve-outs, tighten the permitted-disclosure scope, or add a return/destruction provision?
